label: hate
rationale: 'Stupid luos' attributes collective stupidity to the Luo community; 'Mtapigwa hadi akili ikuje' is a direct threat of violence against the group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Luos are FOOLS' is a blanket collective degradation of the Luo community as a group, not criticism of any individual.

label: hate
rationale: 'We the tribal kikuyu bigots' frames the entire community as collectively tribalist bigots, transferring the label from named politicians onto the ethnic group as a whole.

label: hate
rationale: 'Watu wa nyanza' targets the community as a group and 'mbwa nyinyi' is a dehumanising slur directed at them collectively, with an implied confrontational command.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus' are mockingly told Ruto is their 'dawa' (medicine/punishment), framing the whole community as deserving collective comeuppance - NCIC-level contempt directed at the group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Mikundu' and 'akili zenu wote kama maiti' are crude personal abuse aimed at named individuals (SAMIA, WAMBURA, MAFWELE) and a political system, not an ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Ni nguruwe tu hampendi' calls Muslims pigs - classic dehumanisation of a religious community as a group.

label: neither
rationale: Criticism of a named government official (Gachagua) and his administration; no ethnic group is targeted.

label: neither
rationale: Calling on Ruto to dismiss named ministers is straightforward political criticism with no ethnic dimension.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kazi ya Wamunyoro ni fitina, ukabila na chuki' attributes tribalism, scheming and hatred as inherent collective characteristics of the Munyoro community - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kenya was liberate from grip of kikuyus' and 'wakikuyu mko na uchungu' frame Kikuyus collectively as oppressors who controlled national resources - transfers political critique onto the community.

label: hate
rationale: 'KIKUYUS THINK' and 'you tribal bigots' collectively assign tribalist scheming to Kikuyus as a group, not to named individuals.

label: hate
rationale: 'MURSIK IONJE MWARUBAINE' frames Kalenjin cultural identity (mursik) being displaced by bitter medicine at State House, framing Kalenjin governance as toxically ethnic - 'KALENJIN MAFIA' labels the community as a criminal cabal.

label: neither
rationale: 'kasongo ni masaibu tu' is a negative opinion of a named individual politician, not an attack on an ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'nugu ici' (these monkeys/apes in Kikuyu) is a dehumanising slur directed at the Kikuyu community collectively.

label: hate
rationale: 'How kikuyus voted... portrays them as very greedy' attributes collective greed as an immutable characteristic of the Kikuyu community as a whole.

label: neither
rationale: Observes a Luo-Luhya political rivalry and Babu Owino's personal ambitions - ethnic-bloc political analysis without contempt or collective degradation.

label: hate
rationale: 'Punish Kikuyus by voting against them' frames electoral participation as collective punishment of the Kikuyu community, not policy opposition - ethnic group is the explicit target.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus have singlehandedly made sure nothing good ever comes out of western' holds the entire Kikuyu community collectively responsible for blocking another region's development - NCIC collective blame.

label: hate
rationale: 'A Kisii can't be President... it is a waste of human resources' declares the entire Kisii community categorically unfit to lead - mirrors ex11 (Kalenjin presidency) in structure and NCIC standard.

label: offensive
rationale: 'GOON', 'WAR CRIMINAL', genocide allegations are severe personal attacks on Ruto and named associates as individuals, with no ethnic community targeted.

label: neither
rationale: 'A Kalenjin Never Again' is borderline but the post immediately pivots to Ruto's individual conduct in 2007/08 PEV - the criticism targets him as a named actor, not the Kalenjin community as a whole; confidence reduced for the opening phrase.

label: neither
rationale: Historical analysis of presidential succession patterns naming specific leaders; framing is about elite power-sharing arrangements, not collective contempt for either community.

label: hate
rationale: 'This Kalejins Don't know what they are doing with government' attributes collective incompetence in governance to the Kalenjin community as a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Watch kikuyus so well, they'll use and dump you like rubbish' attributes collective treachery and disposability of allies as an inherent group characteristic of Kikuyus.
